armourRule: Roll 2D6 vs. the player's Armour Value. If the result meets or exceeds the AV, the armour is broken — make an Injury Roll. If the result is lower, the armour holds and no further effect occurs.

modifiers.applies: Armour or Injury roll (choose after rolling)
Armour or Injury roll (choose after rolling)
Any natural Armour roll of 8+ automatically breaks armour

injury.desc: The player is immediately Stunned. Place them face-down on the pitch. They will be turned face-up (but not activated) at the start of your next team turn.

The player is Knocked Out. Remove them from the pitch and place them in the Knocked-out box of their dugout. At the start of each drive they may attempt to return — roll a D6 on a 4+, they return to the Reserves Box.
The player suffers a Casualty. Remove them from the pitch and place them in the Casualty box of their dugout. The opposing Coach now makes a Casualty Roll (D16) against them.

stunty.desc: The player is immediately Stunned. Place them face-down on the pitch.
The player is Knocked Out. Remove them from the pitch and place them in the Knocked-out box.
The player suffers a Casualty. In League Play, no Casualty Roll is made — they automatically suffer the Badly Hurt result (no long-term effect, but miss the rest of this game).
The player suffers a Casualty. Remove them from the pitch and place them in the Casualty box. The opposing Coach now makes a Casualty Roll (D16) against them.

casualty.desc: The player suffers no long-term effects. They miss the rest of this game but are fully recovered for the next.
The player must miss their next game. In League play, they cannot play in the next fixture — mark them as MNG (Miss Next Game) on the Team Roster.

The player suffers a Niggling Injury and must miss their next game. In League play, mark them as MNG and add a Niggling Injury to their record — this permanently applies -1 to all future Casualty rolls made for this player.
The player suffers a permanent Characteristic reduction and must miss their next game. Roll on the Lasting Injury table to determine which characteristic is reduced by 1.

The player is dead! Remove them permanently from the Team Roster. An Apothecary may attempt to bring them back — roll a D6: on a 4+, they survive but suffer a Serious Injury instead; on a 1-3, they are gone forever.
